annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

content.text: {"number":58298,"title":"[CognitiveServices] Docs: Update CHANGELOG and README for 1.6.0-beta.1 new features","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices` **Service directory:** `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/` **Triggered by:** PR #57510 / commit 8ba2ed9 by `@azure-sdk` (version bumped to 1.6.0-beta.2 in #58296) ## What Changed PR #57510 upgraded the API version to `2026-01-15-preview` and introduced several new types and properties: - New **`FoundryAutoUpgrade`** model – configures auto-upgrade behavior for AI Foundry accounts (fields: `Mode`, `PlannedByMicrosoft`, `StatusReason`, `ScheduledOn`) - New **`FoundryAutoUpgradeMode`** enum (`Enabled`, `Disabled`, etc.) - New **`CognitiveServicesQuotaScopeType`** enum - **`CognitiveServicesAccountProperties`** gained three new properties: `FoundryAutoUpgrade`, `AllowProjectManagement`, `DefaultProject` - **`CognitiveServicesManagedNetworkSettingsResource`** received ~100 lines of new operations - `ModelSkuCapacityProperties` and `ServiceAccountUsage` each gained new properties ## Gaps Found - The `1.6.0-beta.1` CHANGELOG entry only says _"Upgraded api-version to 2026-01-15-preview"_ – none of the new types (`FoundryAutoUpgrade`, `FoundryAutoUpgradeMode`, `CognitiveServicesQuotaScopeType`) or new properties on existing models are mentioned - `README.md` **Examples** section contains only a link to general management samples; there are no package-specific code snippets for creating/managing a Cognitive Services account - `README.md` **Key concepts** section links to general Azure SDK docs but does not list or describe the resource types surfaced by this package (accounts, deployments, projects, connections, RAI policies, managed network settings, etc.) - None of the AI Foundry-related features added since v1.5.0 (Projects, Connections, CapabilityHosts) are described or demonstrated in the README 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1 – `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/CHANGELOG.md` Edit the `1.6.0-beta.1` section to list the new types and properties: ```markdown ## 1.6.0-beta.1 (2026-04-17) ### Features Added - Upgraded api-version to `2026-01-15-preview`. - Added `FoundryAutoUpgrade` model and `FoundryAutoUpgradeMode` enum to configure AI Foundry auto-upgrade behavior on a Cognitive Services account. - Added `CognitiveServicesQuotaScopeType` enum for quota scope classification. - Added `FoundryAutoUpgrade`, `AllowProjectManagement`, and `DefaultProject` properties to `CognitiveServicesAccountProperties`. - Enhanced `CognitiveServicesManagedNetworkSettingsResource` with additional management operations. - Added new properties to `ModelSkuCapacityProperties` and `ServiceAccountUsage`. ### Other Changes - Upgraded dependent Azure.Core to 1.53.0. - Upgraded dependent Azure.ResourceManager to 1.14.0. ``` #### File 2 – `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/README.md` Update the **Key concepts** section to enumerate resource types, and replace the generic **Examples** section with a concrete snippet placeholder. Replace the current `## Key concepts` section with: ```markdown ## Key concepts The `Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices` library manages the following resource types: | Resource | Description | |---|---| | `CognitiveServicesAccountResource` | Top-level Cognitive Services account | | `CognitiveServicesAccountDeploymentResource` | Model deployment within an account | | `CognitiveServicesProjectResource` | AI Foundry project scoped under an account | | `CognitiveServicesConnectionResource` | Connection attached to an account or project | | `CognitiveServicesCapabilityHostResource` | Capability host attached to an account | | `CognitiveServicesManagedNetworkSettingsResource` | Managed virtual network settings for an account | | `RaiPolicyResource` / `RaiBlocklistResource` | Responsible AI policy and blocklist resources | | `CommitmentPlanResource` | Commitment plan for reserved capacity | ``` Replace the current `## Examples` section with: ```markdown ## Examples ### Create a Cognitive Services account ``` ``` ``` (The snippet tooling will fill in the code once a sample test is written – see Step 2.) ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets Create a new test file at `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/tests/Samples/Sample1_CognitiveServicesAccount.cs`: ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using System.Threading.Tasks; using Azure.Core.TestFramework; using Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices.Models; using Azure.ResourceManager.Resources; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices.Tests.Samples { public class Sample1_CognitiveServicesAccount : SamplesBase { [Test] [SyncOnly] [Ignore("Only verifying that the sample builds")] public async Task CreateCognitiveServicesAccount() { #region Snippet:CognitiveServices_CreateAccount // Authenticate and get a subscription ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync(); // Get (or create) a resource group ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = (await subscription.GetResourceGroups() .GetAsync("myResourceGroup")).Value; // Define account properties var accountData = new CognitiveServicesAccountData(AzureLocation.EastUS) { Kind = "OpenAI", Sku = new CognitiveServicesSku("S0"), Properties = new CognitiveServicesAccountProperties { CustomSubDomainName = "my-openai-account", PublicNetworkAccess = ServiceAccountPublicNetworkAccess.Enabled, } }; // Create the account CognitiveServicesAccountCollection accounts = resourceGroup.GetCognitiveServicesAccounts(); ArmOperation operation = await accounts.CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, "myAccount", accountData); CognitiveServicesAccountResource account = operation.Value; Console.WriteLine($"Created account: {account.Data.Name}"); #endregion } } } ``` Place the snippet placeholder in `README.md` as shown in Step 1. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README currently contains all required sections but some are thin: | Section | Status | |---|---| | Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Present | | Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Present | | Getting started → Authenticate the client | ✅ Present | | Key concepts | ⚠️ Links to generic docs – needs package-specific resource list | | Examples | ⚠️ Links to generic samples – needs at least one inline snippet | | Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | | Next steps | ✅ Present | | Contributing | ✅ Present | Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for tone and formatting. ### Step 4: Validate Run in order – each must succeed before proceeding: 1. `dotnet build sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 cognitiveservices` — injects snippet code from the new sample file into README placeholders; verify with `git diff` 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 cognitiveservices` — only needed if public API surface changed (it did not change in this doc-only update) 5. `dotnet format sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24575150103/agentic_workflow) · ● 394.2K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58298","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-17T16:22:45Z","updated_at":"2026-04-17T16:22:45Z"}

arguments.body: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue requests documentation updates for `Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices` 1.6.0-beta.1, specifically to expand the CHANGELOG entry with new types/properties introduced by the `2026-01-15-preview` API version upgrade, and to enrich the README with package-specific resource descriptions and a code snippet example.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices` (1.6.0-beta.1)
**Affected API:** `CognitiveServicesAccountProperties`, `FoundryAutoUpgrade`, `FoundryAutoUpgradeMode`, `CognitiveServicesQuotaScopeType`, `CognitiveServicesManagedNetworkSettingsResource`
**Scenarios:**
- CHANGELOG `1.6.0-beta.1` entry is sparse — lists only the API version bump, omitting new types (`FoundryAutoUpgrade`, `FoundryAutoUpgradeMode`, `CognitiveServicesQuotaScopeType`) and new properties on existing models
- README **Key concepts** section links to generic Azure SDK docs rather than listing package-specific resource types
- README **Examples** section links only to general management samples with no inline package-specific code snippets

**Root ask:** Expand the CHANGELOG entry for 1.6.0-beta.1 and update README with a resource-type table and a concrete `Create a Cognitive Services account` code snippet.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation gap issue, not a runtime bug. The gap was detected automatically when PR #57510 upgraded the API version to `2026-01-15-preview` and introduced several new model types without corresponding CHANGELOG or README updates.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review the CHANGELOG `1.6.0-beta.1` section in `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/CHANGELOG.md` and compare against the diff of PR #57510 to identify all missing entries.
2. Update the README `## Key concepts` section with a table of resource types (`CognitiveServicesAccountResource`, `CognitiveServicesAccountDeploymentResource`, `CognitiveServicesProjectResource`, etc.).
3. Add at least one inline C# snippet (e.g., create account) to the README `## Examples` section and run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 cognitiveservices` to inject it.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Mgmt` — Package name `Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices` starts with `Azure.ResourceManager.`, clearly indicating a management-plane SDK.
- **Service:** `Cognitive - Mgmt` — This is the established service label for Cognitive Services management issues, confirmed by CODEOWNERS line 590 (`# ServiceLabel: %Cognitive - Mgmt`) and the corresponding label existing in the repository (color `#e99695`).
- **Confidence:** High — Package name is explicitly stated in the issue body; both category and service labels are unambiguous.
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %Mgmt` (line 903) — Management catch-all entry; no more specific `%Cognitive - Mgmt %Mgmt` combined entry exists in the file.
- **AzureSdkOwners:** @ArthurMa1978
